description: The dead-ideas registry — every strategy idea this project has tested, with its epitaph. The foundry reads this before every round: new ideas must not descend from dead lineages, and must engage the failure lessons. Deaths are data (philosophy.md: a losing strategy that produces clear answers beats a winning one that doesn't).
failure_lessons: FEE FLOOR: ~0.6% round-trip on $200 positions. Edges under ~1%/trade are structurally dead regardless of hit rate. Confirmed twice: textbook sweep (gross +0.03%/trade, net -0.18%) and weekend_illiquidity_momentum (gross-positive, 49.4% win rate, fee-negative).
CONSTRAINT DOMINANCE: 5 slots, $1k ceiling, 1h/symbol cooldown kill 99.6% of frequent signals. Fitness = edge per constraint slot. Frequency is worthless; amplitude per fire is everything.
FIXED EXITS: +5% target / -3% stop / 24h time exit, checked every 5min. Ideas needing custom exits are inadmissible; ideas whose wins resolve slower than 24h are silently truncated.

CANON LINEAGES DIE: both indicator mean-reversion AND the surviving retail structural plays (documented lineages: capitulation reversal, lead-lag, ORB, volume footprint, weekend effect) tested negative net of fees. The arbitraged-away frontier is past the documented-retail-edge line.
GROSS-POSITIVE IS THE TRAP SHAPE: several ideas found real but too-small structure. The question for a new idea is not 'is there signal' but 'why would the remaining signal exceed 1% per event'.

FADE-SHAPED ENTRIES DIE (r001): every entry that fades a move (wick rejection, overshoot snap, band reversion) has lost against the +5%/-3%/24h exit, which structurally rewards follow-through. Propose continuation/initiation shapes, or argue explicitly why a fade survives an asymmetric exit.

THE GATE SURVIVED, THE ENGINE DIED (r001 exploit direction): drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate scored the best numbers ever tested (43.3% win, gross Sharpe 0.98, -0.33% net) — the self-referential regime gate (null-arm win rate + stop-out cluster via context_keys=['system_state']) carries real information, but the 24-bar breakout engine behind it is too weak. Strong prior: pair BETTER ENGINES with the same meta-gate.
CALIBRATE FIRE RATES (r001): predictions missed by 3x-8x in both directions. State the base-rate arithmetic (how many qualifying events per 288-bar day) rather than intuiting rarity.

HORIZON IS A LEVER (2026-07-17): per-variant tp/sl/time_exit_hours are now honored in both replay and live. The fee floor is fixed per trade, so theses resolving over days targeting 8-20% moves face ~10x better cost ratios than 24h theses targeting 1-2%.

COST IS A DESIGN INPUT (2026-07-17): prefer entries where price comes to the level (limit-friendly) and single-fire selectivity — the denominator of edge-per-slot includes what execution costs, not just what the signal knows.

CONJUNCTIONS MULTIPLY TO ZERO (r002): base-rate arithmetic that multiplies per-condition probabilities as if independent produces fire-rate misses of 4x to infinity when the conjuncts are anti-correlated (high volume x small body is nearly mutually exclusive in crypto candles; entropy jumps of +0.35 bits are impossible when H already sits at 0.95/1.0). Every spec must state WHY its conditions are independent, and any idea whose premise can be checked against the data distribution BEFORE a full gauntlet (entropy ceiling, conjunct correlation) should be — a 5-minute histogram would have killed two of five r002 ideas.

NAMED-TIME COILS DIE (r001-r003, 4 deaths): dead_zone (overnight), options_expiry_pin (08:00 UTC), weekend thinness, asian_london handoff (07:00-09:00). A range break carries no extra directional energy for happening at a named clock time — the calendar lens is 0-for-4 on release-shaped ideas and needs a fundamentally different mechanism to earn another slot.

THE GATE KEEPS WINNING, THE ENGINES KEEP STARVING (r001-r003): the self-referential system-state gate is best-of-round three rounds running on three different engines, and r003's version is the first idea ever to pass its own kill criterion (+1.75%/trade, but n=7). The binding constraint is now ENGINE FIRE RATE: a gated idea needs an ungated engine firing >=1/sym/day for the armed sample to reach statistical decidability inside 930d.

THE GATE ITSELF IS THE BOTTLENECK, NOT ENGINE FIRE RATE (r004 refutes the r001-r003 fix): trend_persistence_regime_gated_engine paired the gate with a momentum-persistence engine that comfortably cleared the prescribed >=1/sym/day (5.17/sym/day raw at premise check) — and the armed sample still collapsed to n=39 over 930d (0.008/sym/day, ~27x under prediction), the worst win rate of any gate-family idea yet (25.6%). Four rounds, four engines, and NO gated idea has ever reached n>=100 armed trades (r001 n=460, r002 n=109/35, r003 n=7, r004 n=39). The bottleneck was never the engine's raw frequency — it is the gate's own conjunctive definition (multiple system_state conditions holding simultaneously) being intrinsically rare. A looser or single-condition gate is required before the family can be tested again.

MICROSTRUCTURE-FROM-OHLCV LENS EXHAUSTED (r001-r004, 4-for-4 dead): wick rejection (r001, fade-shaped), absorption (r002, empty conjunction), gap-fill exhaustion (r003, starved 2000x under prediction), one-sided range expansion (r004, well-sampled n=465 and still net -0.71%/trade, worse than a random large bar). Every candle-geometry shape tried — fade, absorption, gap, one-sided thrust — has failed. The lens needs order-book/footprint data to proceed; OHLCV candle geometry alone is exhausted.

BEHAVIORAL-CALENDAR LENS EXHAUSTED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS (r001-r004, 4-for-4 dead): the round-number price level was tested as a fade (r001, overshoot-snap) and as its deliberate inversion, a stop-cascade continuation (r004, breach-continuation) — both dead, both under/over-fired their base rate by an order of magnitude. Named clock-time coils were tested twice more (options-expiry r002, Asian-London r003) — also dead. No remaining calendar/price-level permutation is untested; the lens is closed absent a genuinely new data source (real order-book or open-interest, not an OHLCV-derived time or price artifact).

THE CEILING IS MEASURED (2026-07-18, instrument-exemption ML study): walk-forward gradient boosting with full feature-engineering hindsight tops out at gross +0.02% to +0.2%/trade for horizons of 1h-12h — the sub-12h OHLCV search space is EMPTY below the 0.6% cost floor, and sub-12h theses are inadmissible unless their entire mechanism is cost engineering. At 24h the ceiling reaches gross ~+0.6% (net ~breakeven taker, ~+0.25% maker): the only admissible OHLCV territory is 24h+ horizons with maker-friendly entries — or leaving OHLCV for the Layer-2 context data now being recorded.

LITERATURE PRIORS (2026-07-18, external survey): no published crypto effect clears US-retail-spot costs at hours-to-days horizons — converging with the measured ceiling from outside. Slippage is VOLATILITY-CONDITIONAL (worst when event signals fire; flat slippage assumptions flatter event strategies). Funding signals are crash-risk premia, not free alpha (BIS); directional funding-extreme rules are untested folklore. The open bands: maker execution at 24h, and 1-8 WEEK holds (published persistence; amortizes the floor; unmeasurable in our archive - the live/shadow channel is the instrument there).

SHOCKS MEAN-REVERT AT MULTI-DAY HORIZON (2026-07-19, measured control arm): sign-aligned forward returns after 3%/3x-vol shock bars are -3.0% (24h), -4.3% (72h), -3.3% (120h), n=53 full population — impulse-chasing entries (r002 R0, r003 post_shock, r004 hawkes lineage) lost not because signals were absent but because the true multi-day signal is CONTRARIAN. A multi-day shock-FADE spec is admissible; it must survive the standard premortem+gauntlet and respect the volatility-conditional slippage prior (costs spike exactly at these events). Retest adverse selection is CONFIRMED both-arms (+0.98% never-retested vs -0.89% retested): maker-band entries must come from populations where waiting is not adverse selection.

ideas.name: bollinger_family
ideas.result: all negative net; best n>100 variant -$0.03/trade gross before fees ate it
ideas.epitaph: Mean reversion at 5-min crypto scale exists but is smaller than fees. Also structurally mismatched to asymmetric +5/-3 exits, which want follow-through, not fades.

ideas.name: macross_family
ideas.result: all negative or zero-fire; slow variants fired 0-4 times in 6 months
ideas.epitaph: Crossovers either fire constantly into chop (fee bleed) or so rarely there is no sample. No parameterization escaped the dilemma.

ideas.name: liquidation_cascade_reclaim
ideas.epitaph: 4-sigma cascades with confirmed reclaims are so rare the filters starve the sample; what fired lost. The reclaim confirmation may arrive after the snap-back is spent.

ideas.name: btc_leads_alt_lag_capture
ideas.epitaph: The residual 5-min lead-lag is either gone or the freshness filters are stricter than the phenomenon. n=3 in 6 months is an idea that no longer occurs.

ideas.name: dead_zone_range_break
ideas.epitaph: Overnight coils exist and break, but breaks resolve under the fee floor as often as over it. The compression-release ratio didn't clear costs.

ideas.name: volume_thrust_regime_shift
ideas.tested: 2026-07-16 · 180d constrained replay (gauntlet) · LIVE A/B arm since 2026-07-16
ideas.note: Live as an A/B arm against null, expected negative; promotion bar is beating null at p<0.05 over 100+ closed trades.

ideas.name: weekend_illiquidity_momentum
ideas.tested: 2026-07-16 · 180d constrained replay (gauntlet) · LIVE A/B arm since 2026-07-16
ideas.note: The gross-positive/fee-negative exemplar. Live to test whether live behavior matches backtest.

ideas.name: entropy_collapse_impulse
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (stop-rate >> target-rate). CALIBRATION: fired ~3x the predicted rate (3,283 candidates vs ~1,160 predicted) — the 15th-percentile coil is far more common than theorized, meaning low-entropy states are the norm, not a rare coiled spring. Entropy breaks are noise breaks at this bar scale.

ideas.name: omori_aftershock_ladder
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (net expectancy -0.47% < +0.3%). CALIBRATION: over-fired 2.6x predicted — 4-sigma 'mainshocks' are commoner than seismic intuition suggests (fat tails). The aftershock direction-persistence exists weakly (39.6% vs the asymmetric exit needing ~40%+) but is smaller than fees. Nearest-miss of the shock-family ideas.

ideas.name: failed_auction_rejection_wick
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (net expectancy -0.58% < +0.4%). Wick geometry at fresh extremes does not identify trapped flow — rejections were followed by continuation of the original push as often as reversal, exactly the failure the spec pre-named. Fade-shaped entries keep dying against the +5/-3 asymmetric exit; this is now a LESSON, not just an epitaph.

ideas.name: round_number_overshoot_snap
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion. CALIBRATION: under-fired 5-8x predicted (202 candidates vs ~1,600) — clean overshoot-and-reject at FRESH round levels is rare, and what fired reverted less than fees. Another fade-shaped death.

ideas.name: drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (armed expectancy -0.33% < +0.3%) — but the BEST of round 001 and the best win rate of any non-placebo idea ever tested. The self-referential gate carries real information (gross-positive shape, 43.3% wins against an asymmetric exit); the base breakout signal underneath it is too weak. LESSON for r002: the gate survived, the engine died — try better engines behind the same meta-gate.

ideas.name: conditional_entropy_regime_expansion
ideas.tested: 2026-07-17 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=0 — NEVER FIRED in 2.5 years across 5 symbols
ideas.epitaph: The premise does not exist in the data: 5-min crypto sign sequences already run at H≈0.95 bits against a 1.0-bit ceiling (measured: mean order-2 conditional entropy 0.949, p99 jump +0.02, max ever +0.04 vs the required +0.35). There is no 'predictable regime' to break — the market lives at maximum entropy at this timescale, so an entropy SURGE is mathematically near-impossible, exactly as an entropy COLLAPSE (r001) was commonplace. The entropy lens is now dead in BOTH directions at 5-min resolution; if information theory returns, it must use magnitudes or longer bars, not signs.

ideas.name: epidemic_r0_crossover_ignition
ideas.epitaph: The growth-phase import died like the decay-phase import (Omori): R0>1.5 ignitions of signed volume ARE real events, but they mark the move's midpoint, not its beginning — by the time flow visibly self-amplifies at 5-min resolution the entry is chasing, and the -3% stop harvests the retraces. Continuation entries triggered by volume acceleration have now failed twice (vol_thrust marginal, r0 clearly negative). Even halved costs (maker) recover only a third of the loss: the timing is wrong, not the fees.

ideas.name: absorption_shelf_breakout
ideas.result: n=0 — NEVER FIRED; conjunction empty (of 19,788 windows: 442 pass 6-bar volume, 13 also pass small-body, 0 also pass tight-span)
ideas.epitaph: Absorption is invisible in OHLCV: volume and bar range are strongly POSITIVELY correlated in crypto, so 'high volume + small body' is anti-correlated and demanding it 6 bars running with tight overlap yields the empty set — measured funnel 19788 → 442 → 13 → 0. The spec's base-rate arithmetic multiplied the conjuncts as if independent; they are nearly mutually exclusive. True absorption needs order-book/footprint data (a Layer-2 argument), not candles.

ideas.name: options_expiry_pin_release
ideas.epitaph: The 08:00 UTC release window exists but carries no directional energy on 5-min spot bars: post-settlement breaks of a pre-08:00 pin resolved no better than any coil break (the dead_zone death, relocated to a named timestamp). Fire rate also missed 4x (predicted 0.15/sym/day, actual 0.04) — pins tight enough to qualify are rarer than the spec's arithmetic assumed, and the survivors were ordinary breakouts. Calendar structure alone doesn't make a coil directional; third coil-release death (dead_zone, weekend partial, this).

ideas.name: rejection_streak_gated_ignition
ideas.result: 930d: taker n=109, -$164, -$1.51/slot, win 36.7% (maker -$120, -$1.10/slot). BUT trailing 180d: +$15.24 taker (+0.22%/trade), +$26.30 maker (+0.38%/trade), n=35 — the ONLY stage-1-positive idea of rounds 001-002
ideas.epitaph: Dead by its own criterion over the full window (armed expectancy -0.75% < +0.3%): the saturation gate did not fix the R0 engine's chasing problem across 2.5 years. But the gradient is the loudest yet: positive on the trailing 180d under BOTH fill models, and the gate family is now best-of-round for the SECOND consecutive round (r001 regime gate 43.3% win, r002 gated engine only stage-1 survivor). Either the gate genuinely fits the current regime (testable: it goes on the watchlist for the NEXT 180d, out-of-sample) or best-of-round is what overfitting-to-recency looks like. Small n=35 forbids a claim; the next window will make one.

ideas.name: gap_fill_exhaustion_continuation
ideas.epitaph: 5-min crypto barely gaps: the full conjunction (>0.4% gap + held close + body>median + vol>median) fired 14 times in 2.5 YEARS across 5 symbols — spec predicted 8-15/sym/DAY (~2000x miss), and even the pre-implementation premise check (0.1/day for the raw gap alone) overestimated the conjunction 25x. The nearest-to-flat idea ever tested, so the mechanism is starved rather than refuted — but an engine firing 5x/year cannot feed a 5-slot book regardless of sign. If gaps return, lower the threshold and re-derive the base rate from the data first.

ideas.name: asian_to_london_handoff_thrust
ideas.epitaph: Crypto has no London open at 5-min scale: the Asian-range break during 07:00-09:00 UTC resolved like any other coil break — mostly failed. Fourth named-time coil death (dead_zone overnight, options_expiry 08:00, weekend, this): the clock does not lend a range break directional energy. Fire rate also missed ~50x (one qualifying break per 2.5 weeks, not 0.4/day).

ideas.name: post_shock_multiday_drift
ideas.result: n=35, -$72.49, edge/slot -$2.07, win 28.6%, net -1.04%/trade (kill bar was +0.5%)
ideas.epitaph: There is no multi-day drift after a 3% shock bar here: 71% of shocks hit the -5% stop before the +12% target across 120h — shock direction carries no 5-day signal. Confirms the r002 R0 lesson at a longer horizon: entering ON the visible impulse is chasing, and the horizon lever cannot rescue an entry with no directional edge — HORIZON IS A LEVER only multiplies an edge that exists. CORRECTION (2026-07-19): the control arm overturns the mechanism claim — shock direction DOES carry a multi-day signal, and it is CONTRARIAN (-3 to -4% sign-aligned). The strategy died by trading a real drift backwards, not by the drift's absence. The fade side is the largest gross effect yet measured here and is admissible for a proper spec.
ideas.control_arm: MEASURED 2026-07-19 (fragility re-analysis #2): sign-aligned forward returns after ALL 3%/3x-vol shocks, no barriers: 24h -2.97%+-1.00, 72h -4.32%+-1.31, 120h -3.33%+-1.50 (n=53, cascade clustering understates SEs).

ideas.name: pullback_to_breakout_level_limit
ideas.result: n=178, -$419.04, edge/slot -$2.35, win 30.9%, Sharpe -4.37, net -1.18%/trade — best-powered sample of the round
ideas.epitaph: The retest is not a discount — it is the failure signal. Breakouts that return to the broken level within 2h are disproportionately the ones that are FAILING; the runners you wanted never look back, so waiting at the level systematically selects the losers (win 30.9%, the worst Sharpe ever recorded here). Cost engineering cannot fix adverse selection in the entry: maker fills would recover ~0.2%/trade against a -1.18% expectancy. COST IS A DESIGN INPUT does not mean price-comes-to-you entries are free edge. CONFIRMED (2026-07-19): the control arm was measured and the adverse-selection mechanism holds at ~5 SE.
ideas.control_arm: MEASURED 2026-07-19 (fragility re-analysis #3): raw 24h forward returns, retested-within-2h breakouts -0.89%+-0.25 (n=197) vs never-retested +0.98%+-0.28 (n=128) — ~1.9% gap at ~5 SE. Adverse selection CONFIRMED on both arms.

ideas.name: slot_scarcity_conviction_gate
ideas.result: n=7, +$24.45, edge/slot +$3.49, win 71.4%, net +1.75%/trade — PASSED its kill bar (+0.3%) and beat the ungated engine (-0.16%)
ideas.epitaph: The first idea ever to pass its own kill criterion over the full window — and n=7 forbids believing it (the small-sample trap rule: n<30 means no claim regardless of sign). The gate opened rarely AND its gap engine fires 5x/year, so 2.5 years produced 7 armed trades. The gate family is now 3-for-3 best-of-round on three different engines (r001 regime gate 43% wins, r002 rejection gate stage-1 positive, r003 this) — the self-referential gate concentrates whatever edge exists, and every round the engine under it starves. PRESCRIPTION for the next gate idea: pair the gate with an engine firing >=1/sym/day so the armed sample reaches n>=100 per 930d and the gate's value becomes decidable.

ideas.name: trend_persistence_regime_gated_engine
ideas.result: n=39 armed/placed (74 raw armed-engine candidates), -$96.82, edge/slot -$2.48, win 25.6%, Sharpe -0.73
ideas.epitaph: The raw momentum-persistence engine comfortably cleared the r001-r003 prescription (>=1/sym/day: premise check measured 5.17/sym/day raw), but the ARMED sample still collapsed to n=39 over 930 days (0.008/sym/day, ~27x under the predicted 0.15-0.30 armed rate) — short of the idea's own n>=100 bar for a decisive family-retirement verdict. What did fire produced the worst win rate of any gate-family idea yet (25.6%, vs r001's 43.3% and r002's 36.7%) and a net expectancy of -1.24%/trade, leaving no realistic path to profitability regardless of the sample shortfall. Four consecutive rounds have now paired the self-referential gate with four different engines (r001 n=460, r002 n=109/35, r003 n=7, r004 n=39) and never once reached a statistically decidable armed sample. The r001-r003 prescription — 'pair the gate with a better engine' — is refuted as a fix: the bottleneck was never engine scarcity, it is the gate's own conjunctive definition (multiple system_state conditions required simultaneously) being intrinsically rare.

ideas.name: return_magnitude_compressibility_break
ideas.epitaph: The required pre-implementation premise check correctly confirmed magnitude headroom exists (p99 surprise 4.6, comfortably above the 3.0 threshold) — vindicating 'if information theory returns, use magnitudes not signs' as far as data existence goes. But headroom existing is not the same as the resulting novel-magnitude bar carrying directional continuation edge: the sign of a bar that breaks the recent quiet-magnitude code predicts the following bars no better than the fee floor allows (36.4% win against an exit needing ~40%+). Fired ~7x the predicted rate (0.73/sym/day vs 0.05-0.15) — quiet-then-surprise transitions are far more common in 5-min crypto than the anti-correlation assumption implied. Compressibility break identifies volatility-regime birth; it does not identify which direction that regime resolves.

ideas.name: hawkes_self_excitation_intensity_entry
ideas.epitaph: The idea's central premise — that 'high intensity AND low cumulative displacement' is a rare, anti-correlated conjunction marking a branching birth before a move — was wrong by two orders of magnitude: the premise check found the raw condition true ~29.7/sym/day (200-600x the predicted 0.05-0.15), and the full gauntlet confirmed the same scale (21.4/sym/day). The 'loading phase' the Hawkes import expected to be a brief, informative window is instead close to the market's default state at 5-min resolution, with no bearing on subsequent displacement (37.9% win, no better than entering on the visible impulse). This closes the shock/point-process import family 3-for-3 dead: Omori's decay-phase entry (r001) chased a spent cluster, R0's growth-phase crossing (r002) marked the midpoint, and Hawkes's pre-displacement loading phase (r004) turns out not to be a distinct, tradable regime at all. Timing the entry earlier in the self-excitation lifecycle does not rescue the family.

ideas.name: one_sided_range_expansion_thrust
ideas.epitaph: The engineered inversion worked exactly as designed on the base-rate front — large-body-plus-volume is positively correlated as predicted, the conjunction was populated (not the absorption empty set), and fire rate landed close to the predicted 0.5-1.5/sym/day band (0.68 actual). But populated does not mean informative: a clean one-sided thrust (tiny opposing wick, close pinned at a fresh extreme, elevated volume) continues no better than a random large bar — worse, in fact, than the idea's own named decisive-kill comparison (34.4% win, below the ~38% breakeven reference, and the worst Sharpe of any candidate this round). This is the fourth consecutive microstructure_from_ohlcv death and the best-sampled one yet (wick rejection r001, absorption r002 empty-set, gap-fill r003 starved, this one well-powered and still negative): every OHLCV candle-geometry shape tried — fade, absorption, gap, one-sided expansion — has failed. The lens needs order-book/footprint data to proceed; candle geometry alone is exhausted.

ideas.name: round_number_breach_continuation
ideas.epitaph: The best win rate (42.3%) and only positive Sharpe of the round — another instance of the GROSS-POSITIVE trap shape, not a live edge. The stop-cascade-fuel thesis (a decisive breach of a coiled round-number level recruits resting orders on the far side) is directionally plausible and shows up in the numbers, but the recruited energy is smaller than the fee floor demands (-0.37%/trade net). Fired ~10x the predicted rate (1.08/sym/day vs 0.06-0.15) — coiling near a round level is common, not the rare magnet event assumed. With this death, the round-number price level has now failed in BOTH directions (r001 fade, r004 continuation), closing out the behavioral_calendar lens 4-for-4 dead.
